candidates.text: SHR & OHT LVL TOP BEAM REINFORCEMENT GFC DETAILS

4-Y8,  SIDE FACE REINF. ON BOTH FACES
(2-Y8 ON ONE FACE)

4-Y8,  SIDE FACE REINF. ON BOTH FACES
(2-Y8 ON ONE FACE)
TYPICAL STIRRUP DETAILS

S.F.R.
ON BOTH FACE

DESHUTTERING TIME 
TYPE OF FORM WORK FOR : MINIMUM PERIOD BEFORE STRIKING 
(EXCLUDING THE DAY OF CASTING) 
	a. SLABS UPTO 4.5M 7 DAYS OVER 4.5M 14 DAYS 
	b. BEAMS UPTO 6.0M 14 DAYS OVER  6.0M 21 DAYS
	c. WALL/BEAM/COLUMN VERTICAL FACES  24 HOURS

SHUTTERING CAN BE RELEASED EARLIER THAN SPECIFIED ABOVE PROVIDED THE PROPS ARE 
RETAINED AT REASONABLE SPACINGS AND CONCRETE ATTAINS THE FULL STRENGTH AT THE TIME OF 
DESHUTTERING AS PER IS 456 - 2000. DESHUTTERING SHALL BE DONE BY PROVIDING DAMPENING 
MATERIAL BELOW IN ORDER TO REDUCE THE IMPACT AT THE LOWER LEVEL.
SIMPLY SUPPORTED AND CONTINUOUS SPANS SHALL BE DESHUTTERED FROM MID SPAN 
TOWARDS SUPPORTS. CANTILEVERS SHALL BE DESHUTTERED FROM FREE END TOWARDS SUPPORTS. 
SLAB/BEAMS SHALL BE REPROPPED  UNTIL THE NEXT LEVEL SLAB IS CAST AND DESHUTTERED. 
CONSTRUCTION JOINTS 
SLABS    : AT ONE THIRD OF THE SPAN FROM SUPPORT.
BEAMS   : AT ONE THIRD OF THE SPAN FROM SUPPORT.
 IF ANY CROSS BEAMS ARE LOCATED IN THIS REGION THE JOINT SHALL  BE LOCATED TWO TIMES THE 
 WIDTH OF THE BEAM AWAY FROM THE BEAM. 
RETAINING WALL :  AT THE JUNCTION OF THE WALL AND FOOTING WITH A WALL KEY JOINT AND AT 
                             MIDDLE OF VERTICAL SPAN, JOINTS TO BE AVOIDED. 
RAFT SLAB :  AT ONE THIRD OF THE SPAN FROM SUPPORT. 
                     CONSTRUCTION JOINTS SHALL BE STRAIGHT. 
CANTILEVERS : CONSTRUCTION JOINT SHALL NOT BE PROVIDED IN CANTILEVERS.
WATER STOPPER & NECESSARY MEASURES SHALL BE TAKEN CARE ALL CONSTRUCTION JOINTS 
AS PER RELEVANT IS CODES.








ADMIXTURE
CONCRETE ADMIXTURE SHALL BE USED TO IMPROVE WORKABILITY OR STRENGTH AS 
MANUFACTURER SPECIFICATION.
WATERPROOFING ADMIXTURE SHALL BE USED FOR WATER RETAINING STRUCTURES, PODIUM, TERRACES, AND LIQUID STORAGE STRUCTURES.
GROUT
CEMENT BASED NON-SHRINK GROUT WITH A MINIMUM 7 - DAY CUBE STRENGTH OF 35 N/MM2^ ^SHALL BE USED.
ANCHORE BOLTS
ANCHORE BOLTS GRADE 8.8 MINIMUM, YIELD STRENGTH 640 N/MM2^ REQUIRED. SPECIAL INSPECTION REQUIRED
AND SET ALL ANCORE BOLTS BY TEMPLATE.
MISCELLANEOUS
REFER TO ARCHITECTURAL DRAWINGS FOR WALL OPENINGS, ARCHITECTURAL FEATURES AND DIMENSIONS NOT
 SHOWN.REFER TO MEP DRWINGS FOR SIZE AND LOCATION OF DUCT OPENINGS, PIPING, CONDUITS, ETC. 
NOT SHOWN.REFER TO SEPARATE SPECIFICATIONS FOR INFORMATIONS NOT CONTAINED IN THESE GENERAL NOTES.

1.00   GENERAL
1.01  ALL DIMENSIONS ARE IN SI UNITS. 
1.02  WORK TO FIGURED DIMENSIONS AND DO NOT SCALE THE DRAWING. 
1.03  ALL STRUCTURAL DRAWINGS SHALL BE READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH
        RELEVANT ARCHITECTURAL AND SERVICE DRAWINGS. 
1.04 DIMENSIONS AND CONDITIONS MUST BE VERIFIED IN THE SITE. AND ANY  
        DISCREPANCIES SHALL BE BROUGHT TO THE NOTICE OF THE STRUCTURAL 
        DESIGN TEAM BEFORE PROCEEDING WITH THE AFFECTED PART OF THE  WORK.
1.05  ALL LEVELS OF THE PROJECT REFERS TO ARCHITECTURAL DRAWING. 
1.06  THE STRUCTURE IS DESIGNED FOR : 
         BASEMENT+G+1+TERRACE+HEAD ROOM & OHT FLOORS ONLY.  
1.07 THE STRUCTURE IS DESIGNED TO BE SELF SUPPORTING AND STABLE AFTER THE  
        BUILDING IS COMPLETE. IT IS 
        THE CONTRACTORS RESPONSIBILITY TO DETERMINE ERECTION PROCEDURES
        AND SEQUENCE TO INSURE SAFETY OF THE BUILDING AND ITS COMPONENTS  
        DURING ERECTION. THIS INCLUDES THE ADDITION OF NECESSARY SHORING, 
        SHEETING, TEMPORARY BRACING, GUYS, OR TIE DOWNS.
1.08 PROPOSED CHANGES OR SUBSTITUTIONS TO STRUCTURAL DETAILS OR PLANS 
        SHALL BE SUBMITTED TO THE  DESIGN ENGINEER FOR REVIEW AND APPROVAL.
1.09 STRUCTURE IS CONVENTIONAL SYSTEM FROM FOUNDATION TO SUPER STRUCTURE.

4.06   PROPER SHORT TERM / LONG TERM PRECAUTIONARY MEASURES INCLUDING SHORING, STRUTTING, BRACING  SHALL BE PROVIDED FOR EXCAVATION TO BASEMENT ADJOINING THE NEIGHBORING STRUCTURES TO HOLD EARTH WHERE REQUIRED 100 THICK BINDING AND 150 THICK SOLING WITH SAND CUSHION SHALL BE  PROVIDED UNDER RAFT,INDEPENDENT AND COMBINED FOOTINGS IF REQUIRED DEWATERING SHALL BE DONE BY PROVIDING  SUMPS DEEPER THAN THE GENERAL EXCAVATION FOR RAFT BUT BEYOND THE BUILDING LINES.  

4.07 REFER SEPARATE GENERAL EXCAVATION DRAWING AND SOIL CONSULTANT INPUTS FOR EARTH WORK.
4.08  ALL ABOVE MEASURES TO BE BROUGHT TO THE NOTICE OF SOIL 
           CONSULTANTS  FOR APPROVAL BEFORE EXECUTION.
4.09   REFER SOIL REPORT BY “BORE TECH SERVICES” AND          
          FOR OTHER  RELEVANT INFORMATION ON EXCAVATION / SHORING, REFER  
          SHORING DRAWINGS BY EXTERNAL CONSULTANT.
         
4.10   UNLESS OTHERWISE NOTED, 100 THK. PCC WITH CUBE STRENGTH OF 
          M10 N/MM2 SHALL BE PROVIDED ON ALL SOIL SURFACES FORMING THE  
          BASE FOR STRUCTURAL CONCRETE MEMBERS.
4.11   FOR EXCAVATION BELOW GROUND WITH WATER SEEPAGES, DEWATERING   
          SHALL BE DONE TO KEEP THE EXCAVATION PIT ADEQUATELY  DRY
          DURING FORMING AND CASTING THE FOUNDATION.

CONCRETE SHALL BE OF GRADE M30 WITH MINIMUM CEMENT  
   CONTENT OF 330 kg/Cum.M  AS PER IS : 3370.PART 1
   TANKS SHALL BE TESTED FOR WATER TIGHTNESS 
   AS PER IS :  3370 PART-1.
   ALL STRUCTURAL STEEL EMBEDMENTS , LADDERS ETC , SHALL 
   BE PROVIDED   WITH 2 COATS ANTICORROSIVE PAINT OVER 2 COATS 
   OF PRIMER.
   BACKFILLING AROUND THE WATER TANK SHALL BE DONE AFTER  THE 
   TESTING   OF THE TANK IS COMPLETE WITH THE APPROVAL  	OF  THE
   ENGINEER - IN - CHARGE.
    CARE SHALL BE TAKEN AT ALL TIMES TO ENSURE THAT WATER 	 
    BARS ARE NOT PERFORATED OR DAMAGED IN ANY WAY AND  
    CONCRETE SHALL BE  CAREFULLY PLACED AND COMPACTED 
    AROUND THE WATER BAR TO ENSURE DENSE VOID-FREE  	
    IMPERVIOUS CONCRETE.
   ANY BOLT OR FASTENING EMBEDED IN OR PASSING THROUGH     
   CONCRETE  SHALL BE TO THE APPROVAL OF THE ENGINEER - IN - CHARGE 
   AND SHALL NOT IMPAIR THE WATERTIGHTNESS OF THE STRUCTURE.
   ALL PIPE SLEEVES SHALL BE PROVIDED WITH PUDDLE FLANGES. THE
   CUTTING OR DRILLING OF HOLES IN WATER TANKS IS STRICTLY PROHIBITED.
    ALL THE ABOVE DETAILS REFER QST STANDARD DRAWINGS. FOR MORE 
    CLARITY.

5.00   BASEMENT CONSTRUCTION AND DEWATERING

5.01  IN ORDER TO AVOID FLOATATION, DEWATERING SHALL BE REDUCED ONLY AS THE SUPERSTRUCTURE WEIGHT INCREASES. UNLESS IT CAN BE SHOWN OTHERWISE BY THE CONTRACTOR, THE GROUNDWATER LEVELS SHALL BE MAINTAINED DURING THE CONSTRUCTION AT BELOW THE SOFFIT OF THE FOUNDATION. THE DEWATERING SYSTEM MAY BE REMOVED ONCE THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE STRUCTURE  IS COMPLETED.
5.02  BACKFILLING WITH SELECTED EARTH BEHIND RETAINING WALLS SHALL BEGIN ONLY AFTER THE CONSTRUCTION OF GROUND FLOOR SLAB AND AS APPROVED BY THE ENGINEER.                                                                       
5.03  ANY MAJOR ISSUES RELATED TO DEWATERING SHALL BE BROUGHT TO THE NOTICE OF THE SOIL / SHORING CONSULTANTS AND ALL OTHER PROJECT STAKE HOLDERS BEFORE EXECUTION.

AN INDEPENDENT TESTING LABORATORY SHALL PERFORM THE FOLLOWING TEST  CAST IN PLACE CONCRETE:
A SLUMP OF PORTLAND CEMENT CONCRETE.
A CUBE COMPRESSIVE STRENGTH SHALL BE CONDUCTED FOR EACH GRADE FOR EVERY 50 M3 ^OR PART THEREOF PLACED PER DAY. REQUIRED CUBE(S) SAMPLE QUANTITIES AND TEST AGE AS FOLLOWS:
	1 SAMPLE AT 3 DAYS 
	1 SAMPLE AT 7 DAYS
	1 SAMPLE AT 28 DAYS
ONE ADDITIONAL RESERVE CUBES SHALL BE TESTED UNDER THE DIRECTION OF THE ENGINEER, IF REQUIRED.
MEAN STRENGTH DETERMINED FROM ANY GROUP OF FOUR CONSECUTIVE TEST RESULTS SHOULD BE GREATER OF
	Fck+0.825 x ESTABLISHED STANDARD DEVIATION
	Fck+4 N/mm2 
*  RMC VENDOR SHOULD ACHIEVE TARGET MEAN STRENGTH VALUE OF
             Fck + 1.65 x STANDARD DEVIATION
	Fck+8 N/mm2

8.00   CONCRETE

8.01  MAXIMUM SIZE OF AGGREGATE SHALL BE AS PER MIX DESIGN, HOWEVER   
         PREFERABLY BE 20mm DOWNSIZE AND SHALL BE GRADED.
8.02  PLAIN CEMENT CONCRETE OF MINIMUM M10 GRADE SHALL BE USED.
8.03  MINIMUM CEMENT CONTENT SHALL BE AS PER INDIAN STANDARD CODES  
         WITH RESPECT TO DIFFERENT GRADES OF CONCRETE. 
8.04  REPLACEMENT OF CEMENT WITH FLY ASH / GGBS SHALL BE LIMITED T0  
         30%.
8.05  MAXIMUM FREE WATER CEMENT RATIO - 0.45
8.06  MINIMUM GRADE OF CONCRETE FOR ALL STRUCTURAL MEMBERS- M30 
         OR SPECIFIC ELEMENT DESIGN REQUIREMENT WHICHEVER IS HIGHER.

QST TEAM TO CONFIRM ON THE MATERIAL & PRECAST /RCC SECTIONS & STRUCTURAL STELL SECTIONS, REINFORCEMENT OR ANY OTHER MATERIALS AS APPLICABLE TO THE PROJECT ETC., WITH DIFFERENT STEEL AND CONCRETE GRADES TESTING TO BE DONE AS PER RELEVANT CODES BEFORE START OF THE CONSTRUCTION FOR ANY PART / PORTION OR COMPLETE PROJECT.
CONCRETE SHALL BE PLACED AND CURED ACCORDING TO RELAVANT INDIAN STANDARDS AND SPECIFICATIONS. SUBMIT PROPOSED MIX DESIGN WIH RECENT SITE CUBE OR LAB TESTS FOR REVIEW PRIOR TO USE.

WHERE, FOR ANY REASON, IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO CONFIRM TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF 12.04 ABOVE, LENGTH OF LAPPED SPLICE  AS SPECIFIED SHALL BE INCREASED BY 30%
LAPS FOR REINFORCEMENT IN COMPRESSION IN BEAMS SHALL BE ACCOUNTED ONLY AS STRAIGHT LENGTHS  AND NO HOOKS OR BENDS ARE ACCOUNTED FOR COMPRESSION LAPS OR ANCHORAGES. 
STANDARD HOOKS OR BENDS SHALL BE PROVIDED WHERE NECESSARY IN TENSION REINFORCEMENTS IN BEAMS, COLUMNS, SLABS, ETC.

A) THE CONCRETE GRADE IN BEAMS AND SLABS MEETING AT  	     	     COLUMN SHALL BE THE SAME AS SPECIFIED FOR COLUMN   		     WITHIN AN AREA AROUND THE COLUMN EQUAL TO 4 TIMES THE 	     CROSS - SECTIONAL AREA OF THE COLUMN, SUBJECT TO THE 	     PROVISION THAT THIS GRADE OF CONCRETE SHALL EXTEND 		     ATLEAST FOR A DISTANCE EQUAL TO THE DEPTH OF BEAM OR 	     SLAB FROM THE FACE OF COLUMN, FOR EACH BEAM OR SLAB
	     MEETING AT THE COLUMN.
